description: Soft, naturally sweetened banana snack cakes with a Greek yogurt cream filling, inspired by Little Debbie Banana Twins.

ingredients.item: full-fat Greek yogurt or whipped ricotta

steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly grease a mini loaf pan or muffin tin.
In a large bowl, mash the bananas. Add the applesauce, maple syrup (or honey), egg, and vanilla, and mix well.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. Slowly fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until just combined.
Spoon the batter into the prepared pan, filling each cavity about two-thirds full. Bake for 15-18 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool completely on a wire rack.
Mix the Greek yogurt, maple syrup (if using), and vanilla until smooth for the filling. Refrigerate 10-15 minutes for a thicker filling.
Slice the cooled mini cakes in half lengthwise, spread or pipe the cream filling on the bottom half, and place the top half back on.
